57.reason: [This loop condition causes undefined behavior because it doesn't check if the second integer (exponent) is successfully read, and may lead to infinite loop or reading beyond input. Also, mixing scanf with %d and checking EOF like this is unreliable for the given input format which ends with newline, not EOF.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This loop condition causes undefined behavior because it doesn't check if the second integer (exponent) is successfully read, and may lead to infinite loop or reading beyond input. Also, mixing scanf with %d and checking EOF like this is unreliable for the given input format which ends with newline, not EOF.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This loop condition causes undefined behavior because it doesn't check if the second integer (exponent) is successfully read. If input ends after reading coefficient, the next scanf for exponent may fail, leading to incorrect data or infinite loop. Also, mixing newline-based and EOF-based input handling is inconsistent with problem description which specifies both polynomials end with newline.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The second polynomial input loop uses EOF to detect end, but the problem states input ends with a newline. This causes incorrect reading when input is from a file or pipe without trailing EOF, and may read beyond intended input or hang waiting for more input. It should instead read until newline like the first polynomial.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This loop may read beyond intended input because it doesn't check for newline termination like the first polynomial; it relies on EOF which may not occur in test environments expecting line-based input. Also, after reading the last term of the second polynomial, there's no guarantee that the next character is EOF鈥攖his can cause infinite loop or undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This loop condition causes undefined behavior because it doesn't check if the second integer (exponent) can be read; also, mixing newline-delimited input with EOF leads to incorrect parsing when input ends with newline instead of EOF.]
